# Pull Request

## Description

Briefly describe what this PR does.

## Type of Change

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation update
- [ ] 🧪 Test addition/update
- [ ] 🔧 Refactoring (no functional changes)

## Related Issue

Fixes #<issue_number> (if applicable)

## Testing

Describe the tests you ran:

- [ ] All existing tests pass (`pytest tests/`)
- [ ] New tests added for new functionality
- [ ] Manual testing performed (describe scenario)

Test evidence (screenshots, logs, etc.):

```bash
# Command/output showing tests passing
pytest tests/test_proxy.py -v
```

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's code style
- [ ] I have added type hints to new functions
- [ ] I have added docstrings to new public functions/classes
- [ ] I have updated the documentation (README, CHANGELOG, etc.)
- [ ] I have verified that the build passes (`python -m build`)
- [ ] My commit messages follow Conventional Commits format

## Additional Context

Add any other context, screenshots, or notes about the PR here.

---

**By submitting this PR, I confirm that:**
- This is my own work or I have the right to submit it
- Any AI-assisted code has been reviewed and understood
- I agree to license this contribution under the project's MIT license
